text: Post-activation potentiation (PAP) through appropriate conditioning activities during warm-up can enhance endurance performance.
PAP involves performing high-intensity or resistance-based conditioning activities prior to endurance effort to temporarily enhance subsequent performance.
Monitoring PAP responses during testing, training, and competition can provide useful information for athlete readiness and performance tracking.
The optimal load parameters (intensity, volume, rest interval) for PAP conditioning activities before endurance performance remain unresolved and likely need individualization.
PAP benefits in endurance sports must be balanced against central and peripheral fatigue induced by the conditioning activity, requiring careful management of warm-up intensity and recovery time.
